verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass TM protein with an N-terminal signal peptide and canonical ER-retention signals (KDEL-like motifs), localizing primarily to the ER. However, upon store depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM junctional regions where it directly gates Orai1 and TRPC channels. This ER-to-PM translocation is activation-induced (store-depletion-triggered), dynamic, and constitutes a documented minority pool at the outer leaflet during the Ca2+ signaling cycle. The protein body reaches the outer face as a consequence of organized ER-PM junctional clustering and direct coupling to Orai1; it is not a stable surface resident but qualifies as contextual under dual-localization and activation-induced display.
